title: 'Liberty, prosperity, rule of law'
primary_topics: FLP underlying philosophy as evaluation criterion
sub_topics: P200,000 per scholarship (P100K tuition, P80K stipend, P20K books)
Lord Jay-em Cuntapay (1st place, ADMU, P320K)

stances.claim: FLP scholarships are unique because recipients are judged not only for academic excellence but also for commitment to promote the underlying philosophy of safeguarding liberty and nurturing prosperity under the rule of law.
stances.rhetorical_move: differentiation framing (academic + philosophical commitment as joint criteria)

stances.claim: FLP teaches the indispensability and inseparability of freedom from tyranny AND freedom from poverty, freedom from fear AND freedom from hunger — to be attained 'under the rule of law in a democracy, not under the rule of force in a monocracy.'
stances.rhetorical_move: extended-pair formula (twin-beacons extended to FDR Four Freedoms-style enumeration) + democracy/monocracy contrast

stances.claim: FLP fervently hopes that from the Scholars Society will arise 'the new leaders of our country — patriotic, talented, and trustworthy promoters of liberty and prosperity under the rule of law.'

signature_phrases: justice and jobs; freedom and food; ethics and economics; liberty and prosperity
One is useless without the other
the freedom from tyranny and the freedom from poverty
the freedom from fear and the freedom from hunger
under the rule of law in a democracy, not under the rule of force in a monocracy
decision_framework_signals: applies academic-excellence + philosophical-commitment joint criterion for scholarship eligibility
frames FDR-Four-Freedoms-style extension (freedom from tyranny + poverty + fear + hunger) of twin-beacons formula
uses democracy/monocracy contrast as the regime-context for liberty-and-prosperity attainment
applies scholar-society pipeline framework for generational-leadership cultivation
